---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size50[.center[
Exploring the Success Stories (III)]

- "Green" subsidies addressing environmental problems
]

<br>

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Review submitted cases
    - https://docs.google.com/document/d/1NU_azo7WnABnc2Hz_w_fqu4mfLGEBC-P-ZuhQSIr0yo/edit?usp=sharing




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Paper 2
.size45[
Which of the four policy design options is the "right" choice for addressing your environmental problem?

- Analyze the pros and cons of each option for your chosen problem, and

- Support all claims with evidence
]

???

Keep your eye on the next target: Paper 2.

- In that paper you will be asked to evaluate all four of the approaches we study in class from the perspective of your chosen environmental problem.

### Any questions on the prompt?



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size50[**Today: "Green" Subsidy Cases**]

.size45[
Find a real world example of .textblue[**this approach**] being used to .textblue[**successfully**] address an environmental problem .textblue[**similar to the one in your project**].
]

.size45[
.textblue[**Present as an argument**]: This case shows that addressing environmental problem X can be done successfully using "green" subsidies.
]

???

### Does everybody have an argument to present and discuss today?

<br>

Let's review all the cases for APA format.



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size45[
This case shows that using a "green" subsidy we can address environmental problem X.

- What specific, narrow aspect of your problem does this subsidy focus on?

- Which stakeholders does it target? What does it require them to do?

- What institutions support this subsidy?
]

???

Take a moment to reflect on your argument and get ready to present it to other people along these three dimensions?

### Questions on this?

Get to it!

<br>

Let's start with some speed dating to warm up!

Pair off (10 mins): Each person take 5 minutes to present the case argument you brought for today.

<br>

New pairs, do it again! (10 mins)

<br>

Ok, at this point you've heard two different arguments and hopefully gotten some time to reflect on your own case argument.

Now let's try to extract lessons from all of them.

Everybody form groups of (three? four?) and spread out around the room.
- Try to form groups with people you didn't pair off with during the warm-up!



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**"green" subsidy**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

???

Here's the over-arching goal of our group-led evaluation.

Groups compare and contrast each set of answers and help the class extract generalizable lessons to help us evaluate green subsidies.

### Make sense?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**"green" subsidy**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
1) How specifically do these subsidies target the problem? 
]

???

Ok, groups, first prompt.

Compare and contrast your cases in order to help us identify how the subsidies target the problem.

Any environmental problem is likely to be large and multi-faceted, but I'm willing to bet these subsidies are highly targeted.

Explore that as a group and report back to the class.

<br>

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Problem Targeting'

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**"green" subsidy**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
2) What kinds of stakeholders do these subsidies target?
]

???

Ok, groups, second prompt.

Compare and contrast your cases in order to help us identify how the subsidies target the stakeholders.

- Are the subsidies purely punitive or are there also incentives included?

Explore that as a group and report back to the class.

<br>

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Stakeholder Targeting'

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.center[
Identify the .textblue[**characteristics**] associated with .textblue[**successful**] use of a .textblue[**"green" subsidy**] approach to environmental problem-solving]]

.size50[
3) What kinds of local institutions exist to support and/or reinforce these subsidies? 
]

???

Ok, groups, final prompt.

Compare and contrast your cases in order to help us identify how the subsidies fit into the established local system.

- Are the subsidies supported by institutional actors or built into established processes?

Explore that as a group and report back to the class.

<br>

Collect responses *ON BOARD* and discuss: 'Institutional Support'

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/12-1-Green_Subsidies_v2.png')
background-size: 80%
background-position: bottom
class: top, center

.size50[**Best advice / things to remember when designing "green" subsidies?**]

???

*ON BOARD*

Let's work together to build a policy cheat sheet on the board.

If you were designing a "green" subsidy to address an environmental problem what would you need to keep in mind?

- ?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/13-1-fishing.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: center, inverse

.textwhite[.size65[**Adaptive Governance**]]

???

Next week we explore our next policy design, adaptive governance!

Make sure you consolidate and clean up your notes from this week.

You might end up making this central to your paper!
